item.title: Abu Dhabi’s IRH Turns Down Zambian Copper Concentrate Waiver
item.description: The Zambian unit of Abu Dhabi’s International Resources Holding doesn’t plan to export copper concentrate, after again being granted the largest quota in a government waiver to ship the semi-processed form of the metal.

item.title: Foreign Investors Snap Up Turkish Stocks in Fast-Track Trades
item.description: Foreign investors are piling into fast-tracked sales of shares in Turkish companies, seeing pockets of value in a market facing political turmoil at home and fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Rose in Holiday Week to Most Since February
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits rose last week to the highest level since February, potentially reflecting volatility around the Memorial Day holiday.

item.title: Nvidia-Backed Robotics Startup Generalist AI Valued at $2 Billion
item.description: The company raised $400 million in a funding round led by Radical Ventures

item.title: Morgan Stanley Sees Zambia Lift Eurobond Price to Break Impasse
item.description: Morgan Stanley expects Zambia to revise its bond buyback offer higher in a bid to break an impasse with a group of bondholders resisting an initial price tabled almost a week ago.

item.title: Apollo’s Zelter Sees High-Grade Debt Sales Topping US Treasuries
item.description: Investment-grade debt sales will outpace net issuance of US Treasuries this year, Apollo Global Management Inc.’s President Jim Zelter said, as the Magnificent Seven tech companies and others seek massive amounts of funding to fuel their expansion.

item.title: Nasdaq 100 Futures Drop as Broadcom Forecast Spooks Traders
item.description: A selloff in technology stocks pushed US stocks lower before the bell on Thursday, as results from Broadcom Inc. forced an unwind in the artificial-intelligence trade that had powered gains over the last several sessions.

item.title: Blackstone BCRED Joins Private Credit Funds Limiting Redemptions
item.description: Blackstone Inc. limited redemptions from its flagship private credit fund for the first time after investors sought to pull 10% of the shares, the latest such fund to cap withdrawals amid a continued investor exodus.

item.title: Sugar Futures Climb as Brazilian Mills Seen Making More Ethanol
item.description: Raw sugar futures in New York advanced for the first time three days on expectations that Brazilian mills may allocate more cane to ethanol production, reducing sugar output during the current crushing season.

item.title: Dwindling US Oil Inventories Are a Warning to Global Markets
item.description: The supply buffer is becoming uncomfortably thin as summer driving season begins.

item.title: Schneider Electric Banks on AI Boom to Sell €800 Million Debt
item.description: Schneider Electric SE is planning to sell €800 million ($930 million) of debt following a powerful rally on the back of its increasing incursion into the business of supporting data centers.

item.title: Rand Swings From Carry Loser to Winner as Bond Inflows Surge
item.description: South Africa’s rand is back near the top of the carry-trade table after languishing close to the bottom in the weeks following the outbreak of the Iran war.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 4, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.5% at 7:47 a.m. in New York as AI-linked stocks falter after a disappointing outlook from chipmaker Broadcom.

item.title: UK and France Finalize Postwar Hormuz Mine-Clearing Mission
item.description: The UK and France have finalized plans to lead a multinational mine-clearing mission in the Strait of Hormuz within days of an agreement between the US and Iran to reopen the waterway, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Zimbabwe Says China’s Huayou Plans Lithium Carbonate Plant
item.description: China’s Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt Ltd. plans to set up a lithium carbonate plant in Zimbabwe, according to the southern African country’s mines minister.

item.title: Higher Losses Are Coming to Credit, Pimco’s Ivascyn Warns
item.description: The first sustained default cycle in credit in many years has already started and the market will see higher losses than it’s grown used to, Pacific Investment Management Co.’s chief investment officer warned.

item.title: UK Job Cut Notices Surge to Highest Since 2020 as Iran War Bites
item.description: The number of British workers that face losing their jobs jumped to its highest in more than five years, the latest sign that the labor market is deteriorating amid the economic effects of conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Cancer Drugmaker Parabilis Seeks $475 Million in US IPO
item.description: Parabilis Medicines, a clinical-stage cancer drug developer, is seeking to raise $475 million in an initial public offering.

item.title: KPMG Australia Fallout Widens as RBA and Pension Giant Reassess
item.description: Australia’s central bank and one of the country’s biggest pension funds are scrutinizing and distancing themselves from KPMG Australia as whistleblower misconduct allegations trigger a widening crisis for the Big Four accounting firm.

item.title: Zurich Insurance Expands Data-Center Offering Beyond the US
item.description: Zurich Insurance Group Plc is widening its offering for data-center projects to Europe and South America as it seeks to profit from the global buildout of infrastructure linked to artificial intelligence.

item.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Fall as Broadcom Miss Disrupts AI Trade
item.description: Emerging-market equities headed for the sharpest drop in almost three weeks as Asian technology heavyweights retreated after a disappointing outlook from Broadcom Inc. raised doubts about the blistering artificial-intelligence rally.

item.title: Denmark to Study Whether Wegovy Can Get More People Working
item.description: The new government in Novo Nordisk A/S’s home country of Denmark wants to test whether weight-loss drugs can help get more people into work, adding a new economic dimension to the debate over obesity treatments.

item.title: Revolut Co-Founder, CTO Vlad Yatsenko to Step Down From Role
item.description: Revolut Ltd.’s chief technology officer, Vlad Yatsenko, and Chief Executive Officer Nik Storonsky’s early partner, is stepping down from his position at the digital bank in July.

item.title: The Mortgage Hedging ‘Beast’ Is Returning to the Treasury Market
item.description: At the height of the US bond-market selloff last month, Vishal Khanduja detected something unusual.

item.title: Microsoft’s AI Chief Says Anthropic Models Are Too Expensive
item.description: The software company’s AI chief is working to build cheaper in-house models

item.title: Ramp Notches $44 Billion Valuation in New Funding Round
item.description: Ramp, a corporate spending management platform, has raised $750 million in a new funding round at a $44 billion valuation, a sign of investor enthusiasm for using artificial intelligence to help companies with their bookkeeping.

item.title: Polymarket Lets You Bet on Vibes
item.description: By taking bets on social media clout, the prediction market has made itself vulnerable to bots.

item.title: Logistics Costs in US Surge to a Four-Year High
item.description: It’s a challenge for the Fed to combat inflation fueled by supply shocks

item.title: US Youth Job Prospects May Not Be as Bad as They Seem
item.description: The optimist’s take on the outlook for young workers in the age of AI.

item.title: Buyout and Debt Funds Struggle With $632 Billion Stockpile
item.description: Private markets firms have been slow to spend hoards of capital raised earlier this decade, sparking tough talks with investors about whether they should get more time to find investments.
